methods.description: This method describes the preparation and experimental conditions for studying the response of N. labradorica to methanotrophs using transmission electron microscopy and isotopic imaging.

steps.description: Specimens showing dark greenish-brown cytoplasm were picked using sable artist brushes under a stereomicroscope immediately after wet sieving the sediment using natural seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: select and prepare N. labradorica specimens from sediment

steps.description: Living specimens had a partly inorganic covering surrounding the test, which was gently removed using fine artist brushes.
Specimens were rinsed twice in filtered artificial seawater to remove any sediment before placing them into the experimental petri dishes.
Specimens were minimally exposed to light during preparation as kleptoplasts are highly light sensitive.
steps.searchable_summary: minimize light exposure during specimen preparation

steps.description: The experiment was conducted for 20 hours with additional time points at 4 and 8 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct experiment with specified time points

steps.description: A pre-experimental phase of 2-4 hours was included for specimen acclimation.
Feeding of M. sedimenti was performed once at the beginning by adding 100 µL of culture to 3 mL of artificial seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: feed specimens with M. sedimenti culture

steps.description: Petri dishes were sealed with Parafilm and covered with aluminium foil, placed in an incubator at 2-3°C.
After incubation, each foraminifer was picked with a sterilised fine artist brush and placed into a fixative solution.

methods.description: This method outlines the process of collecting marine sediment samples and isolating living foraminifera for experimental use.

steps.description: Marine sediment samples were collected using a blade corer during a research cruise.
steps.searchable_summary: collect marine sediment samples with blade corer

steps.description: Sediment was wet sieved to a size range of 250-500 µm to isolate N. labradorica.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve sediment to isolate N. labradorica

methods.description: This method describes the geochemical analysis of pore-water samples to measure δ13C DIC and sulfate concentrations.

steps.description: Pore-water samples were taken from a push corer using rhizons inserted at 1 cm intervals.
Samples were split for δ13C DIC and sulfate measurements, with HgCl2 added to stop microbial activity.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for δ13C DIC and sulfate analysis

steps.description: δ13C DIC was measured using a Thermo Scientific GasBench II coupled to a MAT 253 IRMS.
Sulfate was measured with a Metrohm ion chromatography instrument.
steps.searchable_summary: measure sulfate concentration using ion chromatography
